commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can flip the resolver trust default, update focused tests, and remove duplicate tests; owner review is still needed afterward for the auth and upgrade tradeoffs.; Needs attention: No dependency or CI supply-chain changes were found, but the raw-instance-ID default trust path is a concrete gateway identity security concern.; Review finding: [P1] Default to not trusting raw instance ID... (sha=d20df68c10ba274cd0dc3d63ef799af5390c28a4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes gateway node identity handling so signed custom node IDs flow through pairing, registry, catalog/listing, pending work, events, invoke-result checks, and node-host restart state.

PR surface: Source +281, Tests +944. Total +1225 across 30 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main sends `--node-id` as `client.instanceId`, but registry, pending, event, and invoke-result paths still resolve the node from the device/client ID. I did not run a live repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can flip the resolver trust default, update focused tests, and remove duplicate tests; owner review is still needed afterward for the auth and upgrade tradeoffs.
